comment: Both are defined by the identical criterion (absence of spatial/temporal location or physical embodiment) and both are direct top-level siblings of their source's own physical/perdurant-bearing category. The closest structural pairing in this crosswalk.

Same absence-of-spatiotemporal-location criterion; GFO's explicit worked examples (the number "2", pi) are consistent with, though more mathematically specific than, DOLCE's own "sets and facts... only indicative" framing.

Both formally exhaustive top-level partitions built on the same criterion; SUMO's is a 5-way `disjointDecomposition`, GFO's a 3-way `owl:unionOf` — different internal granularity, same outer criterion.

**Retained as `close-match` after explicit re-justification (2026-07-04 review pass)** — unlike the region/quantity or metaphysical/linguistic-grounding pairs downgraded elsewhere in this batch (`time.md` `mapping-006`/`mapping-008`, `space.md` `mapping-005`/`mapping-006`), both sides here are the *same formal kind*: a reified top-level class, sibling to the source's Time/Space branch, defined by the identical criterion (absence of physical spatiotemporal location). GUM's "metaphorical or qualitative space" phrasing is a framing gloss on the same criterion, not a different criterion the way DOLCE's "region" vs. SUMO's "physical Object" was — hence `close-match`, not `overlap`. `high` confidence, not a hedge value: this is a directly analyzed, confidently-reached conclusion (same criterion, cosmetic framing difference only), not an open sourcing question.

Same absence-of-time-and-space criterion for the Abstract node itself. YAMATO's additional `semi-abstract` intermediate tier has no DOLCE counterpart, but that divergence is already recorded separately (`mapping-006`, `unknown`) rather than diluting this mapping's confidence — the Abstract-to-Abstract correspondence itself is directly and confidently supported by each source's own criterion, not an open sourcing question.
